cpi-all-groups.description: CPI All Groups Index (Original, Australia, Monthly)
cpi-all-groups.note: Monthly CPI all groups index number for Australia. MEASURE=1 (index), INDEX=10001, TSEST=10 (original), REGION=50 (Australia). Source: ABS 6401.0
cpi-all-groups.what_it_measures: Headline price level index across all goods and services categories in Australia
cpi-all-groups.when_to_use: When you want the raw CPI index level rather than percentage change

cpi-annual-change.description: CPI All Groups - Annual % Change (Original, Australia, Monthly)
cpi-annual-change.note: Year-on-year CPI percentage change for Australia. MEASURE=3. Source: ABS 6401.0
cpi-annual-change.what_it_measures: Inflation rate: how much consumer prices have risen compared to the same month last year
cpi-annual-change.when_to_use: Best single number for current inflation rate; use for media-style briefings and economic snapshots

cpi-quarterly.description: CPI All Groups Index (Original, Australia, Quarterly)
cpi-quarterly.note: Quarterly CPI headline figure for Australia. Source: ABS 6401.0
cpi-quarterly.what_it_measures: Quarterly CPI all groups index number for Australia
cpi-quarterly.when_to_use: When quarterly granularity is sufficient and you want the index level

unemployment-rate.note: Seasonally adjusted national unemployment rate %. MEASURE=M13, SEX=3 (persons), AGE=1599 (total), TSEST=20. Source: ABS 6202.0
unemployment-rate.what_it_measures: Percentage of the labour force actively seeking and available for work
unemployment-rate.when_to_use: Best headline labour market indicator; use for monthly snapshots and trend analysis

unemployment-original.note: Original (not seasonally adjusted) national unemployment rate %. Source: ABS 6202.0
unemployment-original.what_it_measures: Raw unemployment rate without seasonal adjustment
unemployment-original.when_to_use: When you need the unadjusted figure, e.g. for year-on-year comparisons

participation-rate.note: Percentage of the civilian population aged 15+ who are in the labour force. PARM_ITEM=M12, SEX=3, AGE=1599, TSEST=20. Source: ABS 6202.0
participation-rate.what_it_measures: Share of working-age population either employed or actively seeking work
participation-rate.when_to_use: Key labour market structural indicator; use alongside unemployment rate for fuller picture

employment-level.description: Employed Persons - National (Seasonally Adjusted, Monthly, thousands)
employment-level.note: Total employed persons (thousands), seasonally adjusted. MEASURE=M1, SEX=3, AGE=1599, TSEST=20. Source: ABS 6202.0
employment-level.what_it_measures: Number of employed persons in Australia (thousands)
employment-level.when_to_use: When you need an absolute count of employed persons rather than a rate

labour-force-size.description: Labour Force Size - National (Seasonally Adjusted, Monthly, thousands)
labour-force-size.note: Total labour force (employed + unemployed), seasonally adjusted (thousands). PARM_ITEM=M9. Source: ABS 6202.0
labour-force-size.what_it_measures: Total persons in the labour force (employed + unemployed, thousands)
labour-force-size.when_to_use: When you need the total labour force size, e.g. for denominator calculations

underemployment-rate.description: Underemployment Rate (Proportion of Labour Force, Seasonally Adjusted, Monthly)
underemployment-rate.note: Underemployed persons as a percentage of the labour force. PARM_ITEM=M23, SEX=3, AGE=1599, TSEST=20. Source: ABS 6202.0
underemployment-rate.what_it_measures: Share of the labour force that is underemployed (working fewer hours than desired)
underemployment-rate.when_to_use: Broader slack measure than unemployment; useful for assessing labour market underutilisation

gdp-chain-volume.description: GDP - Chain Volume Measures (Seasonally Adjusted, $M, Quarterly)
gdp-chain-volume.note: Real GDP in chain volume terms, seasonally adjusted. MEASURE=M1, DATA_ITEM=GPM. Source: ABS 5206.0
gdp-chain-volume.what_it_measures: Real (inflation-adjusted) GDP level in chain volume $M, seasonally adjusted
gdp-chain-volume.when_to_use: When you need the GDP level to compute your own growth rates or examine absolute size

gdp-annual-change.note: Real GDP percentage change from previous year. MEASURE=M2. Source: ABS 5206.0
gdp-annual-change.when_to_use: Best single number for economic growth rate; use for macro snapshots

wage-index.description: Wage Price Index - All Industries, All Sectors (Original, Quarterly)
wage-index.note: WPI index including bonuses, all industries, private+public. MEASURE=1 (index), INDEX=THRPIB, SECTOR=7. Source: ABS 6345.0
wage-index.what_it_measures: Index level of wage price changes across all industries and sectors
wage-index.when_to_use: When you need the WPI index level rather than percentage change

wage-annual-change.description: Wage Price Index - Annual % Change, All Industries (Original, Quarterly)
wage-annual-change.note: Year-on-year WPI growth. MEASURE=3 (pct change from corresponding quarter prev year). Source: ABS 6345.0
wage-annual-change.what_it_measures: Annual wage growth rate across all industries and sectors
wage-annual-change.when_to_use: Key wage pressure indicator; use alongside CPI for real wage comparison

population-national.description: Estimated Resident Population - Australia, All Ages (Quarterly)
population-national.note: ERP persons of all ages, Australia. MEASURE=1, SEX=3 (persons), AGE=TOT. Source: ABS 3101.0
population-national.what_it_measures: Total estimated resident population of Australia
population-national.when_to_use: When you need the national headcount for per-capita calculations or population trend analysis

dwelling-prices-mean.description: Mean Price of Residential Dwellings - Australia (Quarterly, $000s)
dwelling-prices-mean.note: Mean price of all residential dwellings, Australia. MEASURE=5 (mean price), REGION=AUS. Value in thousands of dollars. Source: ABS 6416.0
dwelling-prices-mean.what_it_measures: Mean price of residential dwellings across Australia (thousands of dollars)
dwelling-prices-mean.when_to_use: Housing affordability and price trend analysis; more stable than median or city-level indices

trade-balance.description: Balance on Goods - Australia (Seasonally Adjusted, Monthly, $M)
trade-balance.note: Goods trade balance (exports minus imports), seasonally adjusted. DATA_ITEM=170. Source: ABS 5368.0
trade-balance.what_it_measures: Net balance of goods trade (positive = surplus, negative = deficit), $M
trade-balance.when_to_use: Monthly trade balance indicator; key external accounts measure

goods-exports.description: Total Goods Exports - Australia (Seasonally Adjusted, Monthly, $M)
goods-exports.note: Total goods exports, seasonally adjusted. DATA_ITEM=1000. Source: ABS 5368.0
goods-exports.what_it_measures: Total value of goods exported from Australia ($M)
goods-exports.when_to_use: When you need exports separately from trade balance; use with goods-imports for full picture

goods-imports.description: Total Goods Imports - Australia (Seasonally Adjusted, Monthly, $M)
goods-imports.note: Total goods imports, seasonally adjusted. DATA_ITEM=2000. Note: ABS records imports as negative in this series. Source: ABS 5368.0
goods-imports.what_it_measures: Total value of goods imported into Australia ($M, reported as negative)
goods-imports.when_to_use: When you need imports separately from trade balance

household-spending-change.description: Household Spending - Annual % Change, Total (Seasonally Adjusted, Monthly)
household-spending-change.note: Through-the-year percentage change in total household spending, seasonally adjusted. MEASURE=9, CATEGORY=TOT, TSEST=20. Source: ABS 8501.0
household-spending-change.what_it_measures: Annual growth rate in total household spending across all categories
household-spending-change.when_to_use: Consumer spending momentum indicator; RT (Retail Trade) successor series
